---
title: "Source · Crawford et al. 2017"
subtitle: "African skin-pigmentation GWAS (Science, PMID 29025994)"
---

## Why this source

Crawford et al. 2017 is the defining GWAS of skin pigmentation in **African** populations
(SLC24A5, MFSD12, DDB1/TMEM138, OCA2/HERC2, and others). It is **not** in the GWAS Catalog
download, so the catalog pull in `01_catalog_pull.qmd` can never recover it — it has to be
added as its own source. This workbook maps Crawford's Table 1 onto the shared schema and
writes one standardized file; the combine step then folds it in.

This is the template for **adding any non-catalog source**: copy this workbook, point it at
a new source table, adjust the mapping, and write `output/sources/<name>_harmonized.csv`.
No existing workbook changes.

## Source quirks to be honest about

- **Build is hg19** (`pos_hg19`), whereas the catalog is hg38 — harmonization keeps `build`
  per row so nothing silently mis-aligns; rsID is the safe join key.
- **Effect allele = the derived allele.** Crawford codes the whole table against one
  allele/beta convention, and the sign needs calibrating against a known locus
  (`rs1426654`-A is light-associated). We keep the reported `beta` as `effect_size` with
  `effect_type = beta`; the sign convention is a documented review item for scoring, not a
  per-locus summary-stats gap, so `needs_sumstats = False`.
- Ancestry is recorded as African (Crawford's discovery cohorts).

```{python}
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "scripts")
import pandas as pd
from harmonize import to_harmonized, const

cw = pd.read_csv("data/sources/crawford2017_table1.tsv", sep="\t",
                 dtype=str, keep_default_na=False)

mapping = {
    "rsid":          "rsid",
    "chr":           "chr",
    "pos":           "pos_hg19",
    "build":         const("hg19"),
    "effect_allele": "derived",      # Crawford's effect is coded on the derived allele
    "other_allele":  "ancestral",
    "effect_type":   const("beta"),
    "effect_size":   "beta",
    "pvalue":        "pvalue",
    "trait":         const("skin pigmentation (Crawford 2017 Table 1)"),
    "ancestry":      const("African (Crawford et al. 2017 discovery cohorts)"),
    "mapped_gene":   "nearest_gene",
    "pubmed":        const("29025994"),
}

craw = to_harmonized(cw, mapping, source="crawford2017",
                     source_version="2017_Table1", needs_sumstats=False)
craw.to_csv("output/sources/crawford2017_harmonized.csv", index=False)
print(f"{len(craw)} Crawford loci -> output/sources/crawford2017_harmonized.csv")
```

## Effect sizes across the African-ancestry loci

Crawford codes every locus with a comparable **β** (unlike the catalog's mixed OR/β), so effect
sizes *can* be ranked here. Each gene's **lead SNP** (most significant) is shown — the reported
tag SNPs are only ~15 genes in tight LD, so ranking raw SNPs would double-count a locus. Marker
size tracks significance; color is the raw **sign** of β (the sign convention is coded on the
derived allele and is a documented review item, so it is *not* asserted as "lighter/darker").

```{python}
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from vizhelpers import neglog10p, DIR_POS, DIR_NEG

d = craw.copy()
d["beta"] = pd.to_numeric(d["effect_size"], errors="coerce")
d["mlogp"] = d["pvalue"].map(neglog10p)
lead = (d.dropna(subset=["beta", "mlogp"])
          .sort_values("mlogp", ascending=False)
          .drop_duplicates("mapped_gene", keep="first")
          .sort_values("beta"))

fig = go.Figure()
# lollipop stems (0 -> β) on the categorical gene axis
sx, sy = [], []
for _, r in lead.iterrows():
    sx += [0, r["beta"], None]
    sy += [r["mapped_gene"], r["mapped_gene"], None]
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=sx, y=sy, mode="lines", line=dict(color="#ccc", width=1.5),
                         hoverinfo="skip", showlegend=False))
fig.add_vline(x=0, line_color="#888", line_width=1)
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=lead["beta"], y=lead["mapped_gene"], mode="markers",
    marker=dict(size=(7 + 0.32 * lead["mlogp"].clip(upper=60)),
                color=[DIR_POS if b > 0 else DIR_NEG for b in lead["beta"]],
                line=dict(width=0.5, color="#333")),
    customdata=lead[["rsid", "chr", "pos", "effect_allele", "other_allele", "beta", "pvalue"]].values,
    hovertemplate=("<b>%{customdata[0]}</b> · %{y}"
                   "<br>chr%{customdata[1]}:%{customdata[2]} (hg19)"
                   "<br>%{customdata[3]} › %{customdata[4]}"
                   "<br>β = %{customdata[5]:.2f} · p = %{customdata[6]}<extra></extra>"),
    showlegend=False,
))
a = lead[lead["rsid"] == "rs1426654"]
if len(a):
    fig.add_annotation(x=a["beta"].iloc[0], y=a["mapped_gene"].iloc[0],
                       text="rs1426654-A = light (calibration anchor)",
                       showarrow=True, arrowhead=0, arrowwidth=0.7, ax=60, ay=0,
                       font=dict(size=9, color="#333"))
fig.update_layout(
    title=f"Skin-pigmentation effect at {len(lead)} African-ancestry loci (lead SNP per gene)",
    xaxis_title="β (Crawford melanin-index units, hg19) — sign coded on the derived allele",
    yaxis_title="", template="simple_white", height=460,
    margin=dict(t=56, l=100, r=40, b=64),
)
fig
```

*No standard errors are reported in Crawford Table 1, so these are point estimates without CIs;
marker size is −log10(p). Sign calibration against `rs1426654` is a documented scoring TODO.*
